전도서 7:1.content: A good name is better than fine perfume; and the day of death better than the day of one’s birth.

전도서 7:2.content: It is better to go to the house of mourning than to go to the house of feasting; for that is the end of all men, and the living should take this to heart.

전도서 7:3.content: Sorrow is better than laughter; for by the sadness of the face the heart is made good.

전도서 7:4.content: The heart of the wise is in the house of mourning; but the heart of fools is in the house of mirth.

전도서 7:5.content: It is better to hear the rebuke of the wise than for a man to hear the song of fools.

전도서 7:6.content: For as the crackling of thorns under a pot, so is the laughter of the fool. This also is vanity.

전도서 7:7.content: Surely extortion makes the wise man foolish; and a bribe destroys the understanding.

전도서 7:8.content: Better is the end of a thing than its beginning. The patient in spirit is better than the proud in spirit.

전도서 7:9.content: Don’t be hasty in your spirit to be angry, for anger rests in the bosom of fools.

전도서 7:10.content: Don’t say, “Why were the former days better than these?” For you do not ask wisely about this.

전도서 7:11.content: Wisdom is as good as an inheritance. Yes, it is more excellent for those who see the sun.

전도서 7:12.content: For wisdom is a defense, even as money is a defense; but the excellency of knowledge is that wisdom preserves the life of him who has it.

전도서 7:13.content: Consider the work of God, for who can make that straight, which he has made crooked?

전도서 7:14.content: In the day of prosperity be joyful, and in the day of adversity consider; yes, God has made the one side by side with the other, to the end that man should not find out anything after him.

전도서 7:15.content: All this I have seen in my days of vanity: there is a righteous man who perishes in his righteousness, and there is a wicked man who lives long in his evildoing.

전도서 7:16.content: Don’t be overly righteous, neither make yourself overly wise. Why should you destroy yourself?

전도서 7:17.content: Don’t be too wicked, neither be foolish. Why should you die before your time?

전도서 7:18.content: It is good that you should take hold of this. Yes, also don’t withdraw your hand from that; for he who fears God will come out of them all.

전도서 7:19.content: Wisdom is a strength to the wise man more than ten rulers who are in a city.

전도서 7:20.content: Surely there is not a righteous man on earth who does good and doesn’t sin.

전도서 7:21.content: Also don’t take heed to all words that are spoken, lest you hear your servant curse you;

전도서 7:22.content: for often your own heart knows that you yourself have likewise cursed others.

전도서 7:23.content: All this I have proved in wisdom. I said, “I will be wise;” but it was far from me.

전도서 7:24.content: That which is, is far off and exceedingly deep. Who can find it out?

전도서 7:25.content: I turned around, and my heart sought to know and to search out, and to seek wisdom and the scheme of things, and to know that wickedness is stupidity, and that foolishness is madness.

전도서 7:26.content: I find more bitter than death the woman whose heart is snares and traps, whose hands are chains. Whoever pleases God shall escape from her; but the sinner will be ensnared by her.

전도서 7:27.content: “Behold, I have found this,” says the Preacher, “to one another, to find out the scheme

전도서 7:28.content: which my soul still seeks, but I have not found. I have found one man among a thousand, but I have not found a woman among all those.

전도서 7:29.content: Behold, I have only found this: that God made man upright; but they search for many schemes.”
